architecture.description: OpenClaw is organized around a thin control plane, portable runtimes, and reusable skill packages.
architecture.overview: The studio UI talks to shared services, the gateway executes installs and task orchestration, and connected devices run workloads where they are most effective.

security.description: Credentials stay scoped to the environments that need them, while installs and automation flow through explicit gateway or device targets.
security.title: Security model

architecture.title: Architecture

cli.description: Use the OpenClaw CLI to inspect runtimes, install skills, and automate repetitive operations.

install.description: Installs a skill by id from the active repository.

status.description: Shows the status of your gateway and connected devices.

cli.title: CLI Commands

devices.description: Devices connect edge hardware and remote workers so they can run skills close to the data they need.
devices.label: Devices

gateway.description: The gateway handles runtime access, installs, and automation on the machines that host your agents.
gateway.label: Gateway

manager.description: The manager coordinates skills, instances, and policy from a single control surface.
manager.label: Studio manager

intro.description: OpenClaw is a local-first platform for deploying, managing, and extending AI agents across desktop, servers, and edge devices.

localFirst.title: Local-first control
localFirst.description: Keep agents, runtimes, and credentials close to the machines you trust.

extensibleSkills.title: Extensible skill packs
extensibleSkills.description: Compose reusable skills into installable workflows your team can share.

aiPowered.title: AI-powered automation
aiPowered.description: Coordinate agents, gateways, and devices from a single studio experience.

developerFriendly.title: Developer-friendly runtime
developerFriendly.description: Move between source builds, CLI tools, and packaged runtimes without changing products.

howItWorks.description: OpenClaw separates orchestration, runtime access, and device execution so you can keep control of your workloads while scaling them across environments.
howItWorks.title: How it works

intro.title: Meet OpenClaw

quickstart.description: Follow these three steps to install the gateway, register a device, and deploy a skill in minutes.

installGateway.description: Run the installer on a supported machine so OpenClaw can manage local and remote workloads.
installGateway.title: 1. Install the gateway

installSkill.description: Choose a skill or pack from the marketplace and install it to your gateway or connected device.
installSkill.title: 3. Install a skill

registerDevice.description: Open Claw Studio, add a device, and confirm it reports online before assigning work.
registerDevice.title: 2. Register a device

quickstart.title: Launch your first workflow

skills.description: Skills are reusable capability bundles, and packs combine multiple skills into opinionated workflows.

packs.description: A skill pack groups complementary skills so teams can deploy a whole toolkit with one action.
packs.title: What is a skill pack?

skills.title: Skills & Packs

whatIsSkill.description: A skill packages prompts, tools, permissions, and metadata so the runtime can install and execute it consistently.
whatIsSkill.title: What is a skill?

install.description: Choose the installation path that fits your environment, from one-line scripts to source builds.

cloud.description: Provision a cloud VM when you want a gateway that stays online for your whole team.
cloud.title: Cloud deployment
cloud.warning: Use a fresh Linux host and lock down SSH before exposing the dashboard.

docker.description: Run OpenClaw inside Docker for a reproducible gateway on servers, NAS devices, or homelabs.
docker.title: Docker gateway

npm.description: Install the CLI globally when you already have a modern Node.js toolchain.
npm.title: npm / pnpm

script.description: The fastest path for local development and single-machine setups.
script.title: Installer script

source.description: Clone the repository when you need to contribute, debug internals, or build custom distributions.
source.linkAndRunTitle: 2. Link the CLI and run onboarding
source.title: Build from source

install.title: Installation
